qa-lead handoff — 4 gaps/ambiguities found while writing tests, NOT fixed (tests document current behaviour, some as it.todo/regression-lock):

(1) BUG-ish inconsistency: applyRehabFilter (rehabFilter.ts) resolves a product's active classes via getProductActiveKeys (ingredientParser.ts), which reads ONLY activeIngredients/fullIngredientText — it does NOT read Product.activeTags, unlike buildProductFacts (productFacts.ts) which treats activeTags as authoritative. A product whose active class is confirmed only via the wizard tag (no INCI text) is correctly frozen by generate/dailyView but NOT masked by the RehabWidget's own applyRehabFilter. Confirmed pre-existing: rehabFilter.test.ts's own fixtures already only use activeIngredients, never activeTags. Recommend: either have applyRehabFilter consume ProductFacts/getProductActiveKeys extended to also check activeTags (normalized), or document the divergence intentionally if RehabWidget is meant to be INCI/legacy-only.

(2) Coverage gap, not a bug: procedures.json's botox/fillers/smas_lifting freeze rules target { properties: { massageRequired: true } }, but no class in actives.json declares massageRequired (already logged as an intentional forward-compat gap in the FE-1 log entry). Spec Story 4's 'Botox massageRequired freeze' AC cannot be exercised against any real Product today — left as it.todo in tests/routine-engine/clinical-freeze.test.ts. Will need a massage-style product class (or productType) before it's testable/functional.

(3) Dead-data inconsistency: seasons.json's summer_spf_mandate declares "severity": "avoid", but mandates.ts's collectRequireMandates hardcodes nonSkippable:false for every seasonal require mandate and never reads SeasonRule.severity; validate.ts's placeholder severity mapping only branches on nonSkippable. The declared 'avoid' currently has zero effect — the finding surfaces as 'caution'. tests/routine-engine/seasonal-masks.test.ts locks in the CURRENT (likely unintended) behaviour with an explicit 'gap' describe block so a future fix shows up as an intentional test change, not a silent regression. Needs a product/engineering decision: either wire severity through to nonSkippable, or drop the field from the JSON.

(4) Wording ambiguity, not a behavioural bug: spec Story 4 AC4 says a peel's freeze is active 'on D+14 (rehab end)' and lifts 'on D+15'. The engine's actual (already unit-tested, tech-lead-reviewed) semantics are a half-open [0, rehabDays) window on elapsed calendar days: the LAST frozen day is elapsed=rehabDays-1 and the FIRST free day is elapsed=rehabDays (see entryPoints.test.ts's 'Day 20: 14-day freezes are over' case, and the peel's own untilDate = datePerformed + rehabDays used throughout). That's one day earlier than the spec's D+14/D+15 phrasing suggests, most likely a 1-indexed vs 0-indexed wording mismatch. tests/routine-engine/clinical-freeze.test.ts is written against the engine's real (reviewed) behaviour with a comment explaining the discrepancy — flagging for confirmation, not requesting an engine change.

NEXT STEP options for engineer: (a) FE-6 (plan 8.5) — cycle state machine + application counters + adaptation phases, the largest remaining engine gap (Stories 5/6 fully it.todo in the QA suite); or (b) address gaps (1)/(3) above if product owner confirms they're worth fixing before FE-6; FE-7/FE-8/FE-9/FE-10 UI remain queued behind FE-6 per the existing plan order.

TECH-LEAD REVIEW (2026-07-05): FE-4 core pipeline + FE-5 entry points through FE-10 RehabWidget reviewed (everything shipped since the 2026-07-04 Steps 1-4 ACCEPT). Verdict BLOCKED — 1 BLOCKER, 4 WARNINGS; full detail in progress/routine-engine.md's 2026-07-05 log entry. BLOCKER: resolve.ts tryAdmit picks `primary = violations[0]` (first admitted-partner match) to both select which rule's resolution ladder to walk AND to attribute the freeze/day-split reasonCode/ruleId, while `severity` is computed across ALL violations. When a candidate has simultaneous violations of different severities against different partners (reachable today: AHA vs copper_peptides [avoid, offers separate_days] + AHA vs vitamin_c_pure [caution] on the same shelf), admission order alone decides whether the engine correctly day-splits (citing the right rule) or wrongly freezes while citing the caution rule and never trying the avoid rule's own separate_days option. Safety invariant (no avoid-pair co-schedule) still holds; this is an explainability + over-freezing correctness bug, not a safety leak. Not caught by resolve.test.ts or the determinism/safety property suite. validate.ts/substitute.ts do not share this pattern (they use findViolationsAgainst directly, no primary-narrowing) — confirms the bug is isolated to tryAdmit. tsc clean; jest 39 suites/498 passed+33 todo unchanged (matches engineer's own claimed baseline). WARNINGS: (1) tryAdmit 82 lines / resolvePeriods 77 lines / dailyView.getDailyView 63 lines exceed the 50-line guideline; (2) CycleState/ProductApplicationStats live in types/index.ts but sibling SeasonMaskCache lives in utils/routineEngine/seasonMask.ts — inconsistent placement convention, not wrong; (3) buildDraftSummaryLines has no summary line for non-clinical (pair-rule) freezes — product just vanishes from the Draft Preview After column with no line explaining why; (4) RoutinesScreen's adaptation/frozen/rehab memo has no time dependency, can go stale across the 04:00 boundary if the screen stays mounted. NEXT STEP: engineer fixes the resolve.ts BLOCKER (own-rule ladder selection + correct reasonCode attribution), addresses WARNINGS at discretion, re-verifies with the same tsc/jest commands, hands back to tech-lead.

QA TODO ACTIVATION (2026-07-05, qa-lead): converted all 33 it.todo stubs into real tests now that FE-6..FE-10 shipped. 31 activated / 2 left it.todo (Story 4 Botox massageRequired -- no product class declares it yet; Story 8 Diff View transition screen -- FE-8 shipped no such screen). 12 new test files (cycling-and-adaptation.test.ts rewritten in place; routine-step-card, today-screen, draft-preview-sheet, routines-screen-generation-ux, add-procedure-modal, routines-screen-paused-rows, fitzpatrick-card, product-detail-vitc-infobox, rehab-widget-ui, city-field are new; draft-preview.test.ts and seasonal-masks.test.ts extended in place) -- full breakdown in progress/routine-engine.md's 2026-07-05 'QA TODO ACTIVATION' log entry. Domain-level tests (performDailyCheckIn, applyRoutinePlan, refreshSeasonMaskIfDue/getActiveSeasonMask) run against REAL zustand stores with AsyncStorage's official jest mock, per the task's instructions -- zustand itself was never mocked. CROSS-CHECK against the tech-lead's same-day resolve.ts tryAdmit BLOCKER (primary-violation misattribution for candidates with 2+ simultaneous violations of differing severity against different partners): NONE of the 31 newly-activated tests exercise that code path -- every conflict fixture either uses a single opposing rule/partner or hand-constructs the RoutinePlan/FrozenItem shape directly (typed against the real interfaces) instead of deriving it from generatePlan's admission ladder. Multi-violation admission is only exercised by the pre-existing, unmodified determinism-and-safety.test.ts and the engineer's own resolve.test.ts. Environment finding (not a src/ bug, no source file touched): this project's @testing-library/react-native + react-test-renderer combination under-matches getByText/getByPlaceholderText when two host nodes share identical text/placeholder content (verified via screen.toJSON()) -- worked around with screen.UNSAFE_getAllByProps in the 3 affected suites (add-procedure-modal, product-detail-vitc-infobox, city-field). Verified: tsc clean; jest tests/routine-engine -> 21 suites / 95 passed + 2 todo (97 total); jest src/utils src/services --testPathIgnorePatterns=worktrees -> 26 suites / 442 tests green (baseline intact, untouched); combined jest src/utils src/services tests/routine-engine tests/routines --testPathIgnorePatterns=worktrees -> 49 suites / 548 passed + 2 todo (550 total). NEXT: engineer fixes the resolve.ts tryAdmit BLOCKER (still open, confirmed unaffected by this activation pass), re-verifies, hands back to tech-lead for final FE-8/9/10 UI re-review. After that lands, only the 2 remaining it.todo stubs are open, both pending new product/design decisions (massageRequired product data, Diff View screen scope) rather than engine work.

BLOCKER FIXED (2026-07-05): tryAdmit primary violation now severity-ordered (avoid wins over admission order) — ladder + attribution cite the forcing rule; fail-before/pass-after regression in resolve.test.ts (willow-bark BHA vs vitC[caution]+copper[avoid]). Warnings 1-3 fixed (tryAdmit/resolvePeriods/getDailyView split <=50 lines; SeasonMaskCache placement documented — import cycle forbids types/index.ts; pair-freezes now narrated in summary lines + DraftPreviewSheet paused rows). Warning 4 accepted as documented trade-off. tsc clean; 49 suites / 550 passed + 2 todo. REQUESTING tech-lead re-review of the fix scope (resolve.ts refactor, planApply/DraftPreviewSheet narration).

TECH-LEAD RE-REVIEW (2026-07-05): verified the engineer's fix scope for the resolve.ts BLOCKER + WARNINGS 1-3 from the prior review (fix-scope verification only, per protocol -- did not redo the full FE-4..FE-10 review). Verdict: ACCEPT. Confirmed by direct code reading (not by trusting the engineer's log claims): walkResolutionLadder's `primary = violations.find(v => v.severity === 'avoid') ?? violations[0]` closes both halves of the original finding -- it now drives which rule's resolution ladder is walked (so the actual avoid-severity rule's separate_days option is tried, not skipped) AND which ruleId is cited in the freeze/day-split decision, regardless of partner admission order. Hand-traced the new regression test against the real actives.json pair-rule data and confirmed its admission order genuinely produces a caution-first violations array (vitC-caution then copper-avoid), i.e. it is a real regression test, not a coincidentally-passing one. tryAdmit/resolvePeriods/getDailyView refactors (applyFrequencyCaps, walkResolutionLadder, resolveByDaySplit, ResolveRun/runPeriodPass/retryRelocatedInAm, ProjectionContext/projectRoutine) all confirmed behavior-preserving and <=50 lines. SeasonMaskCache placement now has a concrete documented reason (import cycle via rulesetTypes -> types/index.ts). buildDraftSummaryLines + DraftPreviewSheet now narrate every frozen item, clinical or pair-rule, with distinct correct copy. WARNING 4 (memo staleness) accepted as-is per this reviewer's own original low-severity assessment. Independently re-ran `npx tsc --noEmit` (clean) and `npx jest src/utils src/services tests/routine-engine tests/routines --testPathIgnorePatterns="worktrees"` -> 49 suites / 550 passed + 2 todo (552 total), matching the claimed baseline exactly. No new concerns found in this fix scope. FE-1 through FE-10 are now fully reviewed and ACCEPTED end-to-end -- feature is ready for human PR merge. Remaining open items are product-owner decisions, not engineering work, and do not block merge: massageRequired product class/data (finding 2), spec Story 4 AC4 D+14/D+15 wording vs. the engine's real half-open window (finding 4), and whether to build the seasonal Diff View transition screen (qa-lead's 1 remaining, non-massageRequired it.todo).
